content:
🚀 mercury-agent | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a secure, persistent, and user-controlled AI agent that can run 24/7, assisting users through various channels while maintaining a structured memory and respecting defined permissions.
✨ Key Features: • Permission-hardened tools with shell blocklist and folder-level scoping • SQLite-backed Second Brain memory with auto-extraction and conflict resolution
📖 Summary: Mercury is a soul-driven AI agent designed for continuous operation, offering permission-hardened tools, robust memory, and multi-channel access via CLI or Telegram. It prioritizes user control with a pending approval flow and structured memory management.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ witr | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Debugging and understanding the origin and dependencies of running processes, services, and network connections on a system.
✨ Key Features: • Answers "Why is this running?" • Visualizes process causality • Interactive TUI mode • Explains origin and startup chain • Supports Linux, macOS, Windows, FreeBSD
📖 Summary: witr is a command-line tool and TUI application that visualizes the causal chain of running processes, services, and network connections. It aims to answer the question "Why is this running?" by explicitly showing the origin, startup method, and responsible systems for any given process or service.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 CloakBrowser | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Bypassing bot detection mechanisms on websites to enable automated browsing and web scraping tasks that would otherwise be blocked.
✨ Key Features: • 49 source-level C++ patches for various browser fingerprints (canvas, WebGL, audio, etc.) • `humanize=True` flag for human-like mouse, keyboard, and scroll patterns • Achieves a 0.9 reCAPTCHA v3 score
📖 Summary: CloakBrowser is a stealth-focused Chromium browser designed to bypass bot detection systems. It functions as a drop-in replacement for Playwright and Puppeteer, offering a real Chromium binary with source-level fingerprint patches to appear as a legitimate user browser.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 OpenPostings | JavaScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To centralize and streamline the job search and application process by aggregating job postings from various sources and providing tools to manage applications.
✨ Key Features: • Aggregates job postings from multiple ATS providers into a local database • Filters job postings by search text, ATS, industry, region, country, state, county, and remote mode
📖 Summary: OpenPostings is an open-source job aggregator and application tracking system that consolidates job postings from over 100,000 companies across numerous Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS). It allows users to filter jobs by various criteria and manage their application lifecycle, including assisted workflows for cover letters and candidate selection.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 open-design | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and accelerating the creation of digital prototypes, design systems, and visual assets by integrating local coding agents with a comprehensive library of design resources.
✨ Key Features: • Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design • Supports 16 coding-agent CLIs (e.g., Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Copilot CLI) and BYOK proxy
📖 Summary: Open Design is a local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design, enabling users to generate web, desktop, and mobile prototypes, slides, images, and videos. It leverages a variety of coding agents and design systems to automate and enhance the design process, offering sandboxed previews and multiple export options.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ ai-file-sorter | C++
🎯 Primary Use Case: To automatically organize and rename files on a user's desktop or storage devices using AI, making them easier to find and manage.
✨ Key Features: • Content-aware file organization and renaming • Supports local and remote LLMs (e.g., Gemma 3 4B IT) • Visual LLM backends for image analysis • Analyzes document content for renaming suggestions
📖 Summary: AI File Sorter is a cross-platform desktop application that uses artificial intelligence to intelligently organize and rename files like images, documents, audio, and video. It offers a privacy-first approach by supporting local LLMs and providing preview-based workflows for user-controlled file management.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
